item.title: IRAN CLAIMS IT HAS HIGH-SPEED UNDERWATER-LAUNCHED MISSILES: CNN
item.description: IRAN CLAIMS IT HAS HIGH-SPEED UNDERWATER-LAUNCHED MISSILES: CNN

item.title:
🚨 IRAN CLAIMS UNDERWATER MISSILES, THREATENS STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has high-speed missiles launched from underwater, capable of 100 meters per second. Commander Ali Fadavi claimed only Iran and Russia have this tech and suggested they might be used soon. Analysts see the statements as deterrence signaling, highlighting Tehran’s naval capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas route. CNN has not verified the claims.
item.description:
🚨 IRAN CLAIMS UNDERWATER MISSILES, THREATENS STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has high-speed missiles launched from underwater, capable of 100 meters per second. Commander Ali Fadavi claimed only Iran and Russia have this tech and suggested they might be used soon. Analysts see the statements as deterrence signaling, highlighting Tehran’s naval capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas route. CNN has not verified the claims.

item.title: Iran has deployed about a dozen mines in the Strait of Hormuz, halting oil and LNG exports and pushing energy prices higher. The U.S. Navy has destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels but is not escorting commercial ships. President Trump demanded immediate removal of the mines, warning of military consequences if Iran fails to comply. Iran’s military says oil could reach $200 a barrel.
item.description: Iran has deployed about a dozen mines in the Strait of Hormuz, halting oil and LNG exports and pushing energy prices higher. The U.S. Navy has destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels but is not escorting commercial ships. President Trump demanded immediate removal of the mines, warning of military consequences if Iran fails to comply. Iran’s military says oil could reach $200 a barrel.

item.title:
BLS STRUGGLES WITH DATA COLLECTION AMID STAFF SHORTAGE
The Bureau of Labor Statistics has faced staffing shortages and a federal hiring freeze, forcing reliance on less precise estimation methods for price data. In February, “different-cell imputation” was used in 40% of estimates, up from 9% a year ago. Relief may be coming as the BLS posts job openings for price-data collectors nationwide.
item.description:
BLS STRUGGLES WITH DATA COLLECTION AMID STAFF SHORTAGE

The Bureau of Labor Statistics has faced staffing shortages and a federal hiring freeze, forcing reliance on less precise estimation methods for price data. In February, “different-cell imputation” was used in 40% of estimates, up from 9% a year ago. Relief may be coming as the BLS posts job openings for price-data collectors nationwide.

item.title: MACRON: IT IS OBVIOUS THERE IS A NEED FOR DEFINITION OF MILITARY AND POLITICAL OBJECTIVES ON WAR IN IRAN
item.description: MACRON: IT IS OBVIOUS THERE IS A NEED FOR DEFINITION OF MILITARY AND POLITICAL OBJECTIVES ON WAR IN IRAN

item.title:
GERMAN FM: STRAIT OF HORMUZ BLOCKED, DIPLOMACY ONLY OPTION
German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul says the Strait of Hormuz is currently unnavigable, with no shipping movement and no protection available. He warns the blockage threatens energy security for Asia and Europe and says only diplomacy, not military action, can restore safe passage.
item.description:
GERMAN FM: STRAIT OF HORMUZ BLOCKED, DIPLOMACY ONLY OPTION

German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul says the Strait of Hormuz is currently unnavigable, with no shipping movement and no protection available. He warns the blockage threatens energy security for Asia and Europe and says only diplomacy, not military action, can restore safe passage.

item.title: 🚨 U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND WARNS CIVILIANS IN IRAN TO AVOID PORT FACILITIES WHERE IRANIAN NAVAL FORCES ARE OPERATING
item.description: 🚨 U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND WARNS CIVILIANS IN IRAN TO AVOID PORT FACILITIES WHERE IRANIAN NAVAL FORCES ARE OPERATING

item.title:
U.S. MILITARY MISTAKENLY STRIKES SCHOOL IN IRAN
A preliminary U.S. investigation finds the Feb. 28 Tomahawk missile strike on Shajarah Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab was a targeting error. Outdated data from the Defense Intelligence Agency led Central Command to strike a building formerly part of a nearby naval base, killing at least 175 people, mostly children.
Officials say the error reflects human and procedural failures, not new technology. Satellite imagery and videos confirm U.S. responsibility. The investigation is ongoing, with questions about why data wasn’t verified. President Donald Trump initially blamed Iran but says he will accept the findings.
item.description:
U.S. MILITARY MISTAKENLY STRIKES SCHOOL IN IRAN

A preliminary U.S. investigation finds the Feb. 28 Tomahawk missile strike on Shajarah Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab was a targeting error. Outdated data from the Defense Intelligence Agency led Central Command to strike a building formerly part of a nearby naval base, killing at least 175 people, mostly children.

Officials say the error reflects human and procedural failures, not new technology. Satellite imagery and videos confirm U.S. responsibility. The investigation is ongoing, with questions about why data wasn’t verified. President Donald Trump initially blamed Iran but says he will accept the findings.

item.description: Sources tell the BBC that pupils had to hide under their desks as the incident unfolded.

item.description: OIL PRICES LITTLE CHANGED DESPITE IEA PROPOSAL FOR RECORD RELEASE FROM STRATEGIC STOCKS; BRENT, WTI FUTURES LAST UP ABOUT 3%

item.title:
TRUMP CLAIMS WINNING IN IRAN BUT WAR’S END REMAINS UNCLEAR
President Donald Trump repeatedly says the U.S. is succeeding in Iran, but his objectives and exit plan remain vague, fueling domestic and international concern.
The 12-day conflict has shut the Strait of Hormuz, killed U.S. troops, and sparked Iranian retaliation, yet Trump alternates between claiming victory and threatening further action. U.S. allies, including Germany, stress the need for a clear, limited endgame, while polls show Americans are divided and skeptical of Trump’s decisions.
Criticism also grows over the girls’ school bombing, with Trump blaming Iran despite evidence of U.S. missiles hitting the site, raising questions about accountability and strategy.
item.description:
TRUMP CLAIMS WINNING IN IRAN BUT WAR’S END REMAINS UNCLEAR

President Donald Trump repeatedly says the U.S. is succeeding in Iran, but his objectives and exit plan remain vague, fueling domestic and international concern.

The 12-day conflict has shut the Strait of Hormuz, killed U.S. troops, and sparked Iranian retaliation, yet Trump alternates between claiming victory and threatening further action. U.S. allies, including Germany, stress the need for a clear, limited endgame, while polls show Americans are divided and skeptical of Trump’s decisions.

Criticism also grows over the girls’ school bombing, with Trump blaming Iran despite evidence of U.S. missiles hitting the site, raising questions about accountability and strategy.

item.title:
IEA ANNOUNCES 400M-BARREL RELEASE, TIMING UNCLEAR
The International Energy Agency says 400 million barrels could be released, but details on pace and timing are missing. In 2022, the U.S. peaked at about 1 million barrels/day for a month.
This may be the first time the world releases at full speed, creating supply rates the market has never seen. Analysts and traders remain uncertain how much oil will actually reach markets and how quickly.
item.description:
IEA ANNOUNCES 400M-BARREL RELEASE, TIMING UNCLEAR

The International Energy Agency says 400 million barrels could be released, but details on pace and timing are missing. In 2022, the U.S. peaked at about 1 million barrels/day for a month.

This may be the first time the world releases at full speed, creating supply rates the market has never seen. Analysts and traders remain uncertain how much oil will actually reach markets and how quickly.

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS ‘NOTHING LEFT TO TARGET’ IN IRAN WAR
Donald Trump told Axios the conflict with Iran will end “soon” because there is “practically nothing left to target.” He added, “Any time I want it to end, it will end.”
U.S. and Israeli officials, however, plan at least two more weeks of strikes. CENTCOM reports Iranian drone and missile attacks have dropped, while U.S. strikes destroyed 16 mine-laying boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says operations will continue until all objectives are met.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS ‘NOTHING LEFT TO TARGET’ IN IRAN WAR

Donald Trump told Axios the conflict with Iran will end “soon” because there is “practically nothing left to target.” He added, “Any time I want it to end, it will end.”

U.S. and Israeli officials, however, plan at least two more weeks of strikes. CENTCOM reports Iranian drone and missile attacks have dropped, while U.S. strikes destroyed 16 mine-laying boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says operations will continue until all objectives are met.

item.title:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY
Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.
A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.
item.description:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY

Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.

A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.

item.description: $TSLA - TESLA SHARES HIT TWO-WEEK HIGH, LAST UP 2.6%; CO'S CHINA-MADE EV SALES JUMP 91% IN FEBRUARY

item.title:
🚨NO POLICY MOVE CAN STOP CRUDE’S RISE: CARLYLE ENERGY
Jeff Currie, says no policy move can stop crude’s rise, even with a Middle East ceasefire. Supply-chain disruptions—from misplaced ships, canceled insurance, and slowed output in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and the UAE—will take months to fix. He called oil reserve releases a “minuscule offset” to the ongoing disruption.
item.description:
🚨NO POLICY MOVE CAN STOP CRUDE’S RISE: CARLYLE ENERGY

Jeff Currie, says no policy move can stop crude’s rise, even with a Middle East ceasefire. Supply-chain disruptions—from misplaced ships, canceled insurance, and slowed output in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and the UAE—will take months to fix. He called oil reserve releases a “minuscule offset” to the ongoing disruption.

item.title: ALL HSBC BRANCHES CLOSED IN QATAR UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE TO ENSURE SAFETY OF CUSTOMERS AND STAFF - TEXT MESSAGE TO CUSTOMERS
item.description: ALL HSBC BRANCHES CLOSED IN QATAR UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE TO ENSURE SAFETY OF CUSTOMERS AND STAFF - TEXT MESSAGE TO CUSTOMERS

item.title:
SLB SUSPENDS MIDDLE EAST OPERATIONS, CUTS Q1 REVENUE OUTLOOK
SLB, the world’s largest oilfield-services provider has halted some operations and employee travel in the Middle East due to regional instability. The company said measures will continue until conditions stabilize and it will resume activity gradually.
SLB warned these disruptions will reduce first-quarter revenue, sending its shares down 2.3% in premarket trading.
item.description:
SLB SUSPENDS MIDDLE EAST OPERATIONS, CUTS Q1 REVENUE OUTLOOK

SLB, the world’s largest oilfield-services provider has halted some operations and employee travel in the Middle East due to regional instability. The company said measures will continue until conditions stabilize and it will resume activity gradually.

SLB warned these disruptions will reduce first-quarter revenue, sending its shares down 2.3% in premarket trading.

item.title:
HORMUZ TRAFFIC RISES AS IRAN-LINKED SHIPS MOVE
Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains mostly suspended, but vessel-tracking data shows a slight uptick in Iran- and China-linked traffic, including two sanctioned VLCCs fully loaded with crude.
Eight commercial transits were recorded Tuesday, with four more early Wednesday. Recent incidents, including the Mayuree Naree being hit and another vessel turning back, highlight ongoing missile, drone, and security risks.
Since Feb. 28, 13.7 million barrels of Iranian oil have passed the strait, though many ships avoid AIS tracking, making full monitoring difficult. Most commercial tonnage remains blocked until maritime security improves.
item.description:
HORMUZ TRAFFIC RISES AS IRAN-LINKED SHIPS MOVE

Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains mostly suspended, but vessel-tracking data shows a slight uptick in Iran- and China-linked traffic, including two sanctioned VLCCs fully loaded with crude.

Eight commercial transits were recorded Tuesday, with four more early Wednesday. Recent incidents, including the Mayuree Naree being hit and another vessel turning back, highlight ongoing missile, drone, and security risks.

Since Feb. 28, 13.7 million barrels of Iranian oil have passed the strait, though many ships avoid AIS tracking, making full monitoring difficult. Most commercial tonnage remains blocked until maritime security improves.

item.title: 🇯🇵 JAPAN INDUSTRY MINISTRY SAYS PLAN TO RELEASE OIL FROM NATIONAL RESERVES FROM AROUND LATE MARCH
item.description: 🇯🇵 JAPAN INDUSTRY MINISTRY SAYS PLAN TO RELEASE OIL FROM NATIONAL RESERVES FROM AROUND LATE MARCH

item.title: Farage forgets which country he aims to govern
item.description: In domestic and foreign policy, the British right’s vulnerability is its America-worship

item.title:
IRAN’S SHAHED DRONE PRODUCTION SLOWS BUT STOCKPILES REMAIN
Iran’s production of Shahed-136 drone has slowed due to U.S. and Israeli strikes, but existing stockpiles remain and new drones can be made using simple components. Over 2,100 Shaheds have been fired, damaging oil infrastructure, airports, and military equipment.
While Israel’s defenses have intercepted many, the drones’ sheer numbers strain interceptor supplies. Shaheds are easy to launch with minimal infrastructure, unlike ballistic missiles, whose production is now nearly halted. The Strait of Hormuz remains a key target for Iranian attacks, including drones and mines, threatening global oil flows.
item.description:
IRAN’S SHAHED DRONE PRODUCTION SLOWS BUT STOCKPILES REMAIN

Iran’s production of Shahed-136 drone has slowed due to U.S. and Israeli strikes, but existing stockpiles remain and new drones can be made using simple components. Over 2,100 Shaheds have been fired, damaging oil infrastructure, airports, and military equipment.

While Israel’s defenses have intercepted many, the drones’ sheer numbers strain interceptor supplies. Shaheds are easy to launch with minimal infrastructure, unlike ballistic missiles, whose production is now nearly halted. The Strait of Hormuz remains a key target for Iranian attacks, including drones and mines, threatening global oil flows.

item.title: JUST IN: Israeli intelligence assessment indicates Iran’s new supreme leader was wounded at the start of the war
item.description: JUST IN: Israeli intelligence assessment indicates Iran’s new supreme leader was wounded at the start of the war

item.title:
IRAN ATTACKS TWO SHIPS IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it attacked two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after they ignored warnings.
The Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree and Liberian-flagged Express Rome were struck by Iranian fire, according to Fars News Agency.
The UK Maritime Trade Operations says 13 vessel attacks have occurred in the region since the conflict escalated on Feb. 28, including three today. Iran warned ships linked to the U.S. or Israel could be targeted.
item.description:
IRAN ATTACKS TWO SHIPS IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it attacked two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after they ignored warnings.

The Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree and Liberian-flagged Express Rome were struck by Iranian fire, according to Fars News Agency.

The UK Maritime Trade Operations says 13 vessel attacks have occurred in the region since the conflict escalated on Feb. 28, including three today. Iran warned ships linked to the U.S. or Israel could be targeted.

item.title:
Pinned: RECESSION ODDS FALL TO 28% AS EXPANSION HOLDS
Kalshi traders now see a 28% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, down from recent highs of 34–36%. Wells Fargo says the expansion should continue, supported by services, demographics, and fiscal policy. A major, persistent oil shock would be needed to trigger a recession. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
RECESSION ODDS FALL TO 28% AS EXPANSION HOLDS

Kalshi traders now see a 28% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, down from recent highs of 34–36%. Wells Fargo says the expansion should continue, supported by services, demographics, and fiscal policy. A major, persistent oil shock would be needed to trigger a recession.
kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnb…

item.title:
ISRAEL SEES NO GUARANTEE OF IRANIAN UPRISING
Israel says there is no certainty Iran’s government will collapse despite the U.S.-led bombing campaign, a senior official told Reuters. Civilians remain fearful, and no uprising has emerged, even after the deaths of leaders including Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
Israeli leaders emphasize they cannot force regime change, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying it is ultimately up to Iranians. The U.S. and Israel have not set clear public conditions for ending the conflict, while President Donald Trump says the war will continue until objectives are met.
item.description:
ISRAEL SEES NO GUARANTEE OF IRANIAN UPRISING

Israel says there is no certainty Iran’s government will collapse despite the U.S.-led bombing campaign, a senior official told Reuters. Civilians remain fearful, and no uprising has emerged, even after the deaths of leaders including Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Israeli leaders emphasize they cannot force regime change, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying it is ultimately up to Iranians. The U.S. and Israel have not set clear public conditions for ending the conflict, while President Donald Trump says the war will continue until objectives are met.

item.title: Alternative to HRT for menopausal hot flushes now on NHS
item.description: The non-hormonal daily pill could benefit 500,000 women for whom HRT is not suitable.
